content: Just as English language learners build implicit mastery of vocabulary and rhetorical reasoning through repeated structured reflection on their own writing output, large language models can improve their reasoning accuracy by grounding self-reflection mechanisms in explicit, classroom-style iterative feedback cycles that pair linguistic error correction with logical error correction. This framing turns the abstract computational concept of LLM self-reflection into a familiar, measurable pedagogical process that can be adapted from high school writing curricula.
